title: Derived variables
text: two types derived variables CHMS surveys. types derived variables supported chmsflow. Variable mapping - mapping two variables single variable. Variables derived using math equations. chmsflow calculates complex derived variables using functions referenced variable_details.csv within RecTo section prefix ‘Func::’. variables used function referenced variableStart section prefix ‘DerivedVar::’. example, GFR (gfr) includes Func::calculate_GFR RecTo section; DerivedVar::[lab_bcre, pgdcgt, clc_sex, clc_age] variableStart section, indicates four starting variables (lab_bcre, pgdcgt, clc_sex, clc_age).

title: Derived variables
text: derived variable GFR created chmsflow uses harmonized blood creatine (lab_bcre - µmol/L), ethnicity (pgdcgt - 13 categories), sex (clc_sex - 2 categories), age (clc_age - years) variables across CHMS cycles. Using rec_with_table() can transform derived WHR variable across multiple CHMS cycles create transformed dataset. order derive variables, must load existing custom function associated derived variable Since derived variables based previously transformed variables, want transform derived variable, must also specify base CHMS variables rec_with_table() shown . derived GFR variable, also specify blood creatine (lab_bcre), ethnicity (pgdcgt), sex (clc_sex), age (clc_age) variables.
code: # Function for derived GFR variable calculate_GFR <- function(LAB_BCRE, PGDCGT, CLC_SEX, CLC_AGE) {   GFR <- 0   serumcreat <- 0    if (any(!LAB_BCRE %in% 14:785) || (any(!CLC_SEX %in% c(1, 2)) || any(!PGDCGT %in% 1:13)) || any(!CLC_AGE %in% 3:79)) {     GFR <- haven::tagged_na("b") # GFR is NA if any non-responses found   } else {     serumcreat <- LAB_BCRE / 88.4 # Proceeds without non-responses      if (!is.na(CLC_SEX) && !is.na(PGDCGT) && serumcreat != 0) {       if (CLC_SEX == 2 && PGDCGT == 2) {         GFR <- 175 * ((serumcreat)^(-1.154)) * ((CLC_AGE)^(-0.203)) * (0.742) * (1.210) # female and black       } else if (CLC_SEX == 2 && PGDCGT != 2) {         GFR <- 175 * ((serumcreat)^(-1.154)) * ((CLC_AGE)^(-0.203)) * (0.742) # female and not black       } else if (CLC_SEX == 1 && PGDCGT == 2) {         GFR <- 175 * ((serumcreat)^(-1.154)) * ((CLC_AGE)^(-0.203)) * (1.210) # male and black       } else if (CLC_SEX == 1 && PGDCGT != 2) {         GFR <- 175 * ((serumcreat)^(-1.154)) * ((CLC_AGE)^(-0.203)) # male and not black       }     } else {       GFR <- haven::tagged_na("b") # Handle case where CLC_SEX or PGDCGT is NA or serumcreat is 0     }   }    return(GFR) } library(chmsflow) cycle2_gfr <- recodeflow:::rec_with_table(cycle2, variables = c("gfr", "lab_bcre", "pgdcgt", "clc_sex", "clc_age"), variable_details = variable_details, log = TRUE) ## Using the passed data variable name as database_name ## The variable clc_age was recoded into clc_age for the database cycle2 the following recodes were made: ##   value_to       From rows_recoded ## 1     copy    [3, 80]            4 ## 2    NA::a        996            0 ## 3    NA::b [997, 999]            0 ## 4     <NA>       else            0 ## The variable clc_sex was recoded into clc_sex for the database cycle2 the following recodes were made: ##   value_to   From rows_recoded ## 1        1      1            2 ## 2        2      2            2 ## 3    NA::a      6            0 ## 4    NA::b [7, 9]            0 ## 5    NA(b)   else            0 ## The variable lab_bcre was recoded into lab_bcre for the database cycle2 the following recodes were made: ##   value_to         From rows_recoded ## 1     copy    [14, 785]            4 ## 2    NA::a [9994, 9996]            0 ## title: How to add variables to chmsflow
text: complex derived variables, sometimes necessary create functions within custom function. pack-years, nested function used create intermediate smoking variable used main function. information smoking variable means can found Reference section.
code: pack_years_fun <- function(SMKDSTY, CLC_AGE, SMK_54, SMK_52, SMK_31, SMK_41, SMK_53, SMK_42, SMK_21, SMK_11) {   # Age verification   if (is.na(CLC_AGE)) {     return(haven::tagged_na("b"))   } else if (CLC_AGE < 0) {     return(haven::tagged_na("b"))   }    # PackYears for Daily Smoker   pack_years <-     ifelse(       SMKDSTY == 1,       pmax(((CLC_AGE - SMK_52) * (SMK_31 / 20)), 0.0137),       # PackYears for Occasional Smoker (former daily)       ifelse(         SMKDSTY == 2,         pmax(((CLC_AGE - SMK_52 - (CLC_AGE - SMK_54)) * (SMK_53 / 20)), 0.0137) +           ((pmax((SMK_41 * SMK_42 / 30), 1) / 20) * (CLC_AGE - SMK_54)),         # PackYears for Occasional Smoker (never daily)         ifelse(           SMKDSTY == 3,           (pmax((SMK_41 * SMK_42 / 30), 1) / 20) * (CLC_AGE - SMK_21),           # PackYears for former daily smoker (non-smoker now)           ifelse(             SMKDSTY == 4,             pmax(((SMK_54 - SMK_52) * (SMK_53 / 20)), 0.0137),             # PackYears for former occasional smoker (non-smoker now) who             # smoked at least 100 cigarettes lifetime             ifelse(               SMKDSTY == 5 & SMK_11 == 1,               0.0137,               # PackYears for former occasional smoker (non-smoker now) who               # have not smoked at least 100 cigarettes lifetime               ifelse(                 SMKDSTY == 5 & SMK_11 == 2,                 0.007,                 # Non-smoker                 ifelse(                   SMKDSTY == 6,                   0,                   # Account for NA(a)                   ifelse(                     SMKDSTY == "NA(a)",                     haven::tagged_na("a"),                     haven::tagged_na("b")                   )                 )               )             )           )         )       )     )   return(pack_years) }

title: Adjusted diastolic blood pressure — adjust_DBP
text: function adjusts diastolic blood pressure based respondent's diastolic average blood pressure across six measurements. adjustment made using specific correction factors. adjusted diastolic blood pressure returned numeric value.

title: Adjusted diastolic blood pressure — adjust_DBP
code: adjust_DBP(BPMDPBPD)

title: Adjusted diastolic blood pressure — adjust_DBP
text: BPMDPBPD numeric representing respondent's diastolic average blood pressure (mmHg) across six measurements.

title: Adjusted diastolic blood pressure — adjust_DBP
text: adjusted diastolic blood pressure numeric value.

title: Adjusted diastolic blood pressure — adjust_DBP
text: function calculates adjusted diastolic blood pressure (DBP_adj) based value BPMDPBPD. BPMDPBPD greater equal 0 less 996, adjustment made using formula: DBP_adj = 15.6 + (0.83 * BPMDPBPD). Otherwise, BPMDPBPD non-response value (BPMDPBPD >= 996), adjusted diastolic blood pressure set NA(b), indicating measurement available. adjusted diastolic blood pressure returned final output.

title: Adjusted diastolic blood pressure — adjust_DBP
code: # Example: Adjust for a respondent with average diastolic blood pressure of 80 mmHg. adjust_DBP(BPMDPBPD = 80) #> [1] 82 # Output: 82

title: Estimated glomerular filtration rate (GFR) — calculate_GFR
text: function calculates estimated glomerular filtration rate (GFR) according Finlay's formula, serum creatine mg/dL. calculation takes account respondent's ethnicity, sex, age.

title: Estimated glomerular filtration rate (GFR) — calculate_GFR
code: calculate_GFR(LAB_BCRE, PGDCGT, CLC_SEX, CLC_AGE)

title: Estimated glomerular filtration rate (GFR) — calculate_GFR
text: LAB_BCRE Blood creatine (µmol/L). numeric value 14 785. PGDCGT Ethnicity (13 categories). integer value 1 13. CLC_SEX Sex (Male = 1, Female = 2). integer value either 1 2. CLC_AGE Age (years). numeric value 3 79.

title: Estimated glomerular filtration rate (GFR) — calculate_GFR
text: calculated GFR numeric value. input parameters (LAB_BCRE, PGDCGT, CLC_SEX, CLC_AGE) non-response values (LAB_BCRE >= 996, PGDCGT >= 96, CLC_SEX >= 6, CLC_AGE >= 996) bounds, GFR NA(b).

title: Estimated glomerular filtration rate (GFR) — calculate_GFR
text: function uses serum creatine level (LAB_BCRE) µmol/L calculate estimated GFR. First, checks input parameters non-response values. non-response values found, GFR set NA, function return immediately. Otherwise, proceeds calculation converting serum creatine mg/dL (serumcreat = LAB_BCRE / 88.4). Based respondent's ethnicity (PGDCGT), sex (CLC_SEX), age (CLC_AGE), appropriate formula applied calculate GFR. formula used combination ethnicity sex follows:
code: - Female and Black (PGDCGT == 2, CLC_SEX == 2): GFR = 175 * ((serumcreat)^(-1.154)) * ((CLC_AGE)^(-0.203)) *                                                     (0.742) * (1.210)      - Female and not Black (PGDCGT != 2, CLC_SEX == 2): GFR = 175 * ((serumcreat)^(-1.154)) * ((CLC_AGE)^(-0.203)) *                                                         (0.742)      - Male and Black (PGDCGT == 2, CLC_SEX == 1): GFR = 175 * ((serumcreat)^(-1.154)) * ((CLC_AGE)^(-0.203)) *                                                   (1.210)      - Male and not Black (PGDCGT != 2, CLC_SEX == 1): GFR = 175 * ((serumcreat)^(-1.154)) * ((CLC_AGE)^(-0.203))

title: Estimated glomerular filtration rate (GFR) — calculate_GFR
code: # Example 1: Calculate GFR for a 45-year-old white female with serum creatine of 80 µmol/L. calculate_GFR(LAB_BCRE = 80, PGDCGT = 1, CLC_SEX = 2, CLC_AGE = 45) #> [1] 67.27905 # Output: GFR = 67.27905  # Example 2: Calculate GFR for a 35-year-old black female with serum creatine of 70 µmol/L. calculate_GFR(LAB_BCRE = 70, PGDCGT = 2, CLC_SEX = 2, CLC_AGE = 35) #> [1] 99.94114 # Output: GFR = 99.94114

title: Adjusted total household income — calculate_hhld_income

title: Adjusted total household income — calculate_hhld_income
text: function first calculates weighted household size (hh_size_wt) based respondent's actual household size (DHHDHSZ). uses loop iterate 1 DHHDHSZ assigns weights household member based count. household size () 1, weight 1; 2, weight 0.4; greater equal 3, weight 0.3. weighted household size used adjust respondent's total household income (THI_01) dividing hh_size_wt. adjusted household income (adj_hh_inc) returned final output.

title: Adjusted total household income — calculate_hhld_income
code: # Example 1: Respondent with $50,000 income and a household size of 3. calculate_hhld_income(THI_01 = 50000, DHHDHSZ = 3) #> [1] 29411.76 # Output: 29411.76  # Example 2: Respondent with $75000 income and a household size of 2. calculate_hhld_income(THI_01 = 75000, DHHDHSZ = 2) #> [1] 53571.43 # Output: 53571.43  # Example 3: Respondent with $90000 income and a household size of 1. calculate_hhld_income(THI_01 = 90000, DHHDHSZ = 1) #> [1] 90000 # Output: 90000

title: Waist-to-height ratio (WHR) — calculate_WHR
text: function calculates Waist--Height Ratio (WHR) dividing waist circumference height respondent.

title: Waist-to-height ratio (WHR) — calculate_WHR
code: calculate_WHR(HWM_11CM, HWM_14CX)

title: Waist-to-height ratio (WHR) — calculate_WHR
text: HWM_11CM numeric value representing height respondent centimeters. HWM_14CX numeric value representing waist circumference respondent centimeters.

title: Waist-to-height ratio (WHR) — calculate_WHR
text: numeric value representing WHR: HWM_11CM HWM_14CX provided, function returns WHR (waist circumference divided height). either HWM_11CM HWM_14CX missing, function returns tagged NA (NA(b)) indicating invalid input non-response.

title: Waist-to-height ratio (WHR) — calculate_WHR
code: # Example 1: Calculate WHR for a respondent with height = 170 cm and waist circumference = 85 cm. calculate_WHR(HWM_11CM = 170, HWM_14CX = 85) #> [1] 0.5 # Output: 0.5 (85/170)  # Example 2: Calculate WHR for a respondent with missing height. calculate_WHR(HWM_11CM = NA, HWM_14CX = 85) #> [1] NA # Output: NA(b)

title: Non-HDL cholesterol level — calculate_nonHDL
text: function calculates respondent's non-HDL cholesterol level subtracting HDL cholesterol level total cholesterol level. first checks whether input values LAB_CHOL (total cholesterol) LAB_HDL (HDL cholesterol) less certain thresholds (99.6 mmol/L 9.96 mmol/L, respectively). conditions met, calculates non-HDL cholesterol level; otherwise, sets non-HDL value NA indicate calculation applicable.

title: Non-HDL cholesterol level — calculate_nonHDL
code: calculate_nonHDL(LAB_CHOL, LAB_HDL)

title: Non-HDL cholesterol level — calculate_nonHDL
text: LAB_CHOL numeric representing respondent's total cholesterol level mmol/L. LAB_HDL numeric representing respondent's HDL cholesterol level mmol/L.

title: Non-HDL cholesterol level — calculate_nonHDL
text: numeric representing calculated non-HDL cholesterol level (mmol.L) LAB_CHOL LAB_HDL specified thresholds; otherwise, returns NA(b) indicate calculation applicable.

title: Non-HDL cholesterol level — calculate_nonHDL
text: function calculates non-HDL cholesterol level subtracting HDL cholesterol level total cholesterol level. first checks LAB_CHOL LAB_HDL less specified thresholds (99.6 mmol/L 9.96 mmol/L, respectively). conditions met neither input missing, non-HDL cholesterol level calculated. either conditions met either input missing (NA), function returns NA(b) indicate calculation applicable.

title: Non-HDL cholesterol level — calculate_nonHDL
code: # Example: Respondent has total cholesterol of 50 mmol/L and HDL cholesterol of 5 mmol/L. calculate_nonHDL(LAB_CHOL = 50, LAB_HDL = 5) #> [1] 45 # Output: 45 (non-HDL cholesterol = total cholesterol - HDL cholesterol = 50 - 5 = 45)

title: Cardiovascular Disease (CVD) family history — determine_CVD_family_history
text: function evaluates respondent's family history cardiovascular disease (CVD), based data diagnoses heart disease stroke immediate family members ages diagnoses occurred. identifies premature CVD diagnosis occurred age 60.

title: Cardiovascular Disease (CVD) family history — determine_CVD_family_history
code: determine_CVD_family_history(FMH_11, FMH_12, FMH_13, FMH_14)

title: Cardiovascular Disease (CVD) family history — determine_CVD_family_history
text: FMH_11 Integer: Indicates whether immediate family member diagnosed heart disease. - 1 "Yes" - 2 "". FMH_12 Numeric: Represents youngest age diagnosis heart disease immediate family member. FMH_13 Integer: Indicates whether immediate family member diagnosed stroke. - 1 "Yes" - 2 "". FMH_14 Numeric: Represents youngest age diagnosis stroke immediate family member.

title: Cardiovascular Disease (CVD) family history — determine_CVD_family_history
text: integer indicating CVD family history: 1: "Yes" — Family history premature CVD exists (diagnosis age 60). 2: "" — family history premature CVD. NA(b): Missing/unknown — Due non-responses, invalid inputs, unknown diagnosis ages.

title: Cardiovascular Disease (CVD) family history — determine_CVD_family_history
text: FMH_11 (heart disease history) FMH_13 (stroke history) NA, function returns NA(b). either FMH_11 FMH_13 indicates diagnosis (1 "Yes"), corresponding age (FMH_12 heart disease FMH_14 stroke) evaluated: Ages 0 59 indicate premature CVD. Ages 60 100 indicate late-onset CVD. Ages outside range invalid inputs (997, 998, 999) result NA(b). FMH_11 FMH_13 2 (""), family history CVD (2). invalid inputs FMH_11 FMH_13 (values greater 2) also result NA(b).

title: Cardiovascular Disease (CVD) family history — determine_CVD_family_history
code: # Example 1: Premature CVD due to heart disease diagnosis at age 50 determine_CVD_family_history(FMH_11 = 1, FMH_12 = 50, FMH_13 = 2, FMH_14 = NA) #> [1] 1 # Output: 1

title: Low risk drinking score — low_drink_score_fun
text: integer representing low drink score, : 1 "Low risk" (0 points), 2 "Marginal risk" (1–2 points), 3 "Medium risk" (3–4 points), 4 "High risk" (5–9 points). inputs invalid bounds, function returns tagged NA.

title: Low risk drinking score — low_drink_score_fun
text: scoring determined first allocating points (referred step1) based weekly alcohol consumption respondent's sex: respondent drank past year (ALC_11 == 1): ALCDWKY ≤ 10, assign 0 points. ALCDWKY > 10 ≤ 15: assign 0 points males (CLC_SEX == 1) 1 point females (CLC_SEX == 2). ALCDWKY > 15 ≤ 20: assign 1 point males 3 points females. ALCDWKY > 20: assign 3 points. respondents drink past year (ALC_11 == 2), 0 points assigned. step1 points mapped final categorical score follows: 0 points → score 1 ("Low risk"), 1–2 points → score 2 ("Marginal risk"), 3–4 points → score 3 ("Medium risk"), 5–9 points → score 4 ("High risk").

title: Low risk drinking score - former/never categories — low_drink_score_fun1
text: Step 1: Assign points based weekly alcohol consumption. respondent drank past year (ALC_11 == 1): 0 10 drinks/week: 0 points 11 15 drinks/week: 0 points males, 1 point females 16 20 drinks/week: 1 point males, 3 points females 20 drinks/week: 3 points males, 5 points females drink past year (ALC_11 == 2): 0 points Step 2: Determine final categorical score. point score Step 1 0, final category determined based lifetime past-year drinking habits: score 1 (Never drinker) assigned respondent either never drank alcohol lifetime former drinker regularly consume 12 drinks week. score 2 (Low-risk drinker) assigned respondent drank past year (still scored 0 points) former drinker history regularly consuming 12 drinks week. point score Step 1 1 2, respondent classified Moderate drinker (Score = 3). point score Step 1 3 , respondent classified Heavy drinker (Score = 4).

title: Smoking pack-years — pack_years_fun
text: function calculates individual's smoking pack-years based various CHMS smoking variables. Pack years measure used researchers quantify lifetime exposure cigarette use.

title: Smoking pack-years — pack_years_fun
code: pack_years_fun(   SMKDSTY,   CLC_AGE,   SMK_54,   SMK_52,   SMK_31,   SMK_41,   SMK_53,   SMK_42,   SMK_21,   SMK_11 )

title: Smoking pack-years — pack_years_fun
text: SMKDSTY integer representing smoking status respondent: 1: Daily smoker 2: Occasional smoker (former daily) 3: Occasional smoker (never daily) 4: Former daily smoker (non-smoker now) 5: Former occasional smoker (non-smoker now) smoked least 100 cigarettes lifetime 6: Non-smoker (never smoked 100 cigarettes) CLC_AGE numeric value representing respondent's age. SMK_54 numeric value representing respondent's age stopped smoking daily. SMK_52 numeric value representing respondent's age first started smoking daily. SMK_31 numeric representing number cigarettes smoked per day daily smokers. SMK_41 numeric value representing number cigarettes smoked per day occasional smokers. SMK_53 numeric value representing number cigarettes smoked per day former daily smokers. SMK_42 numeric value representing number days past month respondent smoked least 1 cigarette (occasional smokers). SMK_21 numeric value representing respondent's age first started smoking occasionally. SMK_11 integer representing whether respondent smoked least 100 cigarettes lifetime: 1: Yes 2:

title: Smoking pack-years — pack_years_fun
text: numeric value representing pack years respondent's smoking history. CLC_AGE missing negative, returns tagged_na("b"). different smoking statuses (SMKDSTY), function calculates pack years follows: Daily smoker (1): pmax(((CLC_AGE - SMK_52) * (SMK_31 / 20)), 0.0137) Occasional smoker (former daily) (2): pmax(((CLC_AGE - SMK_52 - (CLC_AGE - SMK_54)) * (SMK_53 / 20)), 0.0137) + ((pmax((SMK_41 * SMK_42 / 30), 1) / 20) * (CLC_AGE - SMK_54)) Occasional smoker (never daily) (3): (pmax((SMK_41 * SMK_42 / 30), 1) / 20) * (CLC_AGE - SMK_21) Former daily smoker (4): pmax(((CLC_AGE - SMK_52 - (CLC_AGE - SMK_54)) * (SMK_53 / 20)), 0.0137) Former occasional smoker (5): SMK_11 == 1 (≥100 cigarettes): 0.0137 SMK_11 == 2 (<100 cigarettes): 0.007 Non-smoker (6): 0 SMKDSTY NA(), returns tagged_na(""). unexpected inputs, returns tagged_na("b").

title: Smoking pack-years — pack_years_fun
code: # Example 1: Age = 40, daily smoker, started smoking at 20, and smokes 30 cigs/day (1.5 packs/day). pack_years_fun(SMKDSTY = 1, CLC_AGE = 40, SMK_52 = 20, SMK_31 = 30) #> [1] 30 # Output: 30 (pack years)  # Example 2: A former occasional smoker who smoked at least 100 cigarettes in their lifetime. pack_years_fun(   SMKDSTY = 5, CLC_AGE = 50, SMK_54 = 40, SMK_52 = 18, SMK_31 = NA,   SMK_41 = 15, SMK_53 = NA, SMK_42 = 3, SMK_21 = 25, SMK_11 = 1 ) #> [1] 0.0137 # Output: 0.0137 (pack years)
